title: UK provisionally records its hottest June day as red heat warnings hit western Europe
summary: The Met Office said the UK reached about 36°C on 24 June, provisionally its highest June temperature on record, as red extreme-heat warnings covered Surrey and South-East England and France and Italy issued alerts.

title: US and Iran agree Hormuz safe-passage line; IMO to evacuate 11,000 seafarers
summary: Mediators Qatar and Pakistan said the US and Iran agreed a communication line for safe shipping through the Strait of Hormuz and a Lebanon de-confliction cell, as the IMO announced an evacuation of more than 11,000 stranded seafarers.

title: Trump cancels housing-bill signing, conditioning it on an elections bill
summary: Hours before a planned ceremony, Trump said he would not sign the bipartisan 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act until Congress passes the SAVE America Act on voter registration.

title: Appeals court lets Trump administration resume nationwide expedited removal
summary: A divided D.C. Circuit panel vacated a lower-court order, allowing fast-track deportation without a hearing for people who cannot prove two years' continuous US presence.

title: Ukraine strikes occupied Crimea; occupation officials report widespread power loss
summary: Ukraine said it destroyed a railway bridge over the North Crimean Canal and hit energy and fuel sites; occupation officials reported widespread blackouts and two people injured in Sevastopol.

title: Micron beats estimates and guides higher; shares jump after the Anthropic AI-memory deal
summary: Micron reported fiscal Q3 revenue of about $41.5bn and adjusted EPS of $25.11, guided to roughly $50bn for the current quarter, and rose about 15% after the close.

title: Study identifies why H5N1 infects dairy cows' udders rather than their lungs
summary: University of Pittsburgh researchers found H5N1 binds N-linked sialic acid receptors abundant in cow udder tissue but scarce in their airways; published in Science Advances.

title: SETI radio search of interstellar object 3I/ATLAS finds no technosignatures
summary: A SETI Institute-led scan detected no artificial radio signals from 3I/ATLAS, with all candidate detections attributed to human-made interference.

title: World Cup: Scotland beaten 2-0 by Brazil and exit; England held by Ghana
summary: Vinícius Júnior scored twice as Brazil beat Scotland 2-0 in Miami on 24 June, ending Scotland's bid for a first World Cup knockout place, a day after England were held 0-0 by Ghana.

title: Wall Street steadies on rotation out of tech; oil and gold fall, Micron jumps after the close
summary: U.S. stocks were mixed at Wednesday's close as the Dow rose and the S&P 500 and Nasdaq slipped; Brent and gold fell on easing Middle East tension and Micron rose about 15% after hours.

title: US and Iran give conflicting accounts of nuclear-inspector access after first Switzerland talks
summary: US Vice-President JD Vance said Iran agreed to readmit IAEA inspectors after the first US–Iran talks in Switzerland; Iran's foreign ministry said no visit to its war-damaged nuclear sites is scheduled.

title: Alan Greenspan, US Federal Reserve chair from 1987 to 2006, dies at 100
summary: Greenspan, who led the Fed for more than 18 years across four presidencies, died on 22 June of complications from Parkinson's disease.

title: Memory-chip shares lead a global equity sell-off; Kospi closes about 10% lower
summary: South Korea's Kospi fell 9.99% and triggered circuit breakers as Samsung and SK Hynix each dropped about 12%; the US Nasdaq closed 2.21% lower.

title: NATO heads to Ankara summit on 5% spending pledge as Czech PM excludes President Pavel from delegation
summary: Ahead of the 7–8 July summit, PM Andrej Babiš left President Petr Pavel off the Czech delegation; Pavel asked the Constitutional Court to rule on who represents the state abroad.

title: Albania protests over a Kushner-linked Adriatic resort enter a fourth week
summary: Demonstrators in Tirana have marched for more than three weeks against a resort planned for the protected Vjosa-Narta coast and Sazan island, demanding PM Edi Rama's resignation.

title: UN chief ties climate and energy crises to fossil fuels in London address
summary: António Guterres used a London Climate Action Week address to call for a faster renewables transition, AI data-centre emissions disclosure and methane cuts.

title: Messi becomes the World Cup's all-time leading scorer with 18 goals
summary: Lionel Messi scored both goals in Argentina's 2-0 win over Austria, passing Miroslav Klose's men's record and Marta's overall mark.

title: Astronomers report the earliest known 'flickering' quasar, 850 million years after the Big Bang
summary: An MIT-led team says brightness changes in quasar J0439+1634 point to a settled accretion disk, suggesting some black holes matured very early.

title: A chip-led sell-off; oil eases on Iran supply, gold slips as Fed-hike bets firm
summary: Wall Street and Asia fell on a memory-chip rout; Brent settled near $75.67 as Washington granted Iran a 60-day oil licence, and gold dropped 1.8%.

title: Starmer announces resignation as UK prime minister; Labour to choose a successor by 1 September
summary: Keir Starmer said on Monday he will step down as Labour leader and prime minister, triggering a leadership process with nominations opening on 9 July; Andy Burnham is the reported front-runner. Sterling held lower and gilt yields edged up.

title: US and Iran conclude first Switzerland session; mediators report ‘encouraging progress’ on sanctions and Hormuz
summary: US and Iranian delegations wrapped up the first session of talks in Switzerland, with mediators Qatar and Pakistan reporting ‘encouraging progress’; Iranian officials said draft oil-sanctions waivers and the release of frozen assets were discussed, and technical teams will continue this week.

title: Italy's Meloni rejects Trump's ‘fabricated’ G7 account; foreign minister Tajani cancels US trip
summary: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni called ‘completely fabricated’ a claim by President Trump that she had begged him for a photo at the G7 summit; Foreign Minister Antonio Tajani cancelled a US visit, in a public rift between the two governments.

title: Poland strips Zelensky of its highest honour over UPA military unit; Ukraine returns the award
summary: Polish President Nawrocki revoked Ukrainian President Zelensky's Order of the White Eagle after a Ukrainian unit was named for the WWII-era UPA; Zelensky and several officials returned Polish honours.

title: WHO warns of “missing” Ebola cases in DR Congo as official tallies diverge across agencies
summary: A month into the Bundibugyo-virus outbreak, the WHO says it is still missing cases amid intense community transmission, while WHO, the CDC and the ECDC publish materially different case counts.

title: Japan proposes G7 jointly stockpile critical minerals; leaders set a 60% cap on reliance on any single supplier
summary: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi pitched a G7 "Joint Stockpiling Cooperation Initiative" at the Evian summit, where leaders agreed to cut dependence on any single non-G7 supplier of rare earths to below 60% by 2030.

title: Aarhus researchers report intense UV light can break down PFAS without added chemicals
summary: A lab study at Aarhus University finds that hydrogen radicals released from water by short-wavelength UV light can strip fluorine from PFAS “forever chemicals,” though degradation is partial and slow.

title: Review concludes nicotine vapes are likely to cause lung and oral cancer
summary: A qualitative risk assessment led by UNSW Sydney in the journal Carcinogenesis judges nicotine e-cigarettes likely carcinogenic, while several outside experts call the conclusion an overinterpretation of the evidence.

title: Stocks mixed as Starmer resigns and US–Iran talks conclude; oil slips below $79
summary: European shares were modestly higher and the pound held lower after UK PM Keir Starmer announced his resignation, while US futures slipped on renewed Iran threats; Brent fell below $79 as the first Switzerland session ended with ‘encouraging progress.’

title: Iran says it has re-closed the Strait of Hormuz; Switzerland talks postponed
summary: After an Israeli strike on Lebanon, Iran says it has again closed the strait and the US–Iran talks are postponed — the week-old memorandum under strain.

title: Burnham wins Makerfield; a Labour leadership contest opens
summary: The Manchester mayor took the seat with about 55% and is expected to press Starmer to stand aside.

title: Ukraine strikes Crimea-bridge approaches overnight; trackers still disagree
summary: Drones hit the Kerch and Kavkaz ports on 21 June; DeepState and ISW give opposite readings of who is gaining ground.

title: The ceasefire's weakest link: Israel and Lebanon
summary: An Israeli strike on Lebanon on Saturday became the flashpoint that pushed Iran to re-close the strait and stall the talks.

title: Congo Ebola: 896 cases, 232 deaths, a global emergency
summary: The WHO's highest alert is in force; the Bundibugyo strain still has no licensed vaccine.

title: AI labs step onto the world stage: G7 debut, Seoul office, new benchmark
summary: Altman, Amodei and Hassabis appeared together at the G7; Anthropic opened a Seoul office and its Fable 5 model topped a hard-maths benchmark.

title: CERN completes the doubly-charmed baryon family
summary: Physicists report the third and final member, a confirmation of the Standard Model.

title: A California fault is more stressed than in 1,000 years
summary: Researchers describe an 'earthquake gate' on a system overdue for a major rupture.

title: A hawkish Fed reset the week before the holiday lull
summary: Markets were shut Friday for Juneteenth; the story remains Warsh's pivot toward a 2026 hike.

title: US and Iran sign the deal: ceasefire locked in, Hormuz blockade lifted
summary: Presidents Trump and Pezeshkian signed a memorandum — at Versailles and in Tehran — to halt fighting on all fronts and reopen the strait.

title: Ukraine sets a Moscow refinery ablaze in record drone strike
summary: Fires nine miles from the Kremlin; 500+ flights disrupted; Zelensky says it is time the war ended.

title: Warsh's Fed turns hawkish — a 2026 hike now on the table
summary: Rates held at 3.50–3.75%, but the easing bias is gone; nine of eighteen officials see a hike.

title: SpaceX buys Cursor maker Anysphere for $60bn
summary: SpaceX stock rose 17% and vaulted above Amazon and Microsoft to the fourth most valuable US firm.

title: Hegseth announces 'NATO 3.0' and a review of US forces in Europe
summary: The defense chief calls allies shameful as a six-month posture review begins.

title: Burnham wins Makerfield, setting up a Starmer showdown
summary: The Manchester mayor's by-election win opens a Labour leadership fight; Starmer vows to stay.

title: Congo Ebola passes 830 cases as WHO warns of worst-ever risk
summary: Nearly 200 dead; the Bundibugyo strain still has no licensed vaccine.

title: Scientists map the planet's hidden fungal 'superhighway'
summary: Underground networks moving about four billion tonnes of CO2 into soils a year, mapped for the first time.

title: G7 courts the AI chiefs as enterprise adoption accelerates
summary: Altman, Amodei and Hassabis join a G7 governance lunch in France.

title: Stocks rebound after the Fed shock; oil steadies near $78
summary: Nasdaq +1.91%, Russell 2000 +2.12%; Brent around $79 as the blockade ends.

title: US and Iran reach initial deal to end war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz
summary: A memorandum of understanding extends the fragile ceasefire by 60 days and reopens the strait.

title: Russian barrage sets Kyiv's thousand-year-old Lavra ablaze
summary: At least 11 killed nationwide; 70 missiles and 611 drones launched overnight.

title: SpaceX completes the largest IPO in history
summary: $75bn raised; shares add another 20% on Monday, with market cap above $2tn.

title: Ebola accelerates in DR Congo: 782 cases, 181 deaths
summary: The Bundibugyo strain has no licensed vaccine; the WHO seeks $518m.

title: Europe reels from 'exceptionally early' heat as AMOC watch grows
summary: 40.3°C in Portugal in May; 2026–30 seen 1.3–1.9°C above pre-industrial levels.

title: China's JUNO detector delivers its first neutrino result
summary: A Nature cover; oscillation precision sharpened 1.6x in just 59 days of data.

title: AI spending reclassified as core infrastructure
summary: JPMorgan earmarks about $19.8bn; new frontier models from OpenAI, Google and Anthropic.

title: Swiss voters reject a 10-million population cap
summary: About 55% say no on 59% turnout, averting a clash with the EU.

title: Carolina Hurricanes win their second Stanley Cup
summary: A 3–0 Game 6 shutout in Las Vegas; the goaltender stops all 22 shots.

title: Wall Street hits records on peace and SpaceX
summary: The Dow closes at a record 51,671; Nasdaq +3.07%; Brent slides toward $84.
